posts.title: Cyberpunk Alley in the Rain
posts.content:
A moody neon alley scene that holds up across aspect ratios. The key is keeping the light sources motivated — every glow comes from signage or puddle reflections.

## Base prompt

```
rain-soaked cyberpunk alley at night, holographic signage in kanji and hangul,
steam rising from street vents, lone figure with transparent umbrella,
reflections in puddles, cinematic lighting, anamorphic lens flare,
shot on Kodak Portra 800 --ar 2:3 --v 7 --style raw
```

## Variations

- Swap `lone figure with transparent umbrella` for `stray cat on a dumpster` for a quieter mood
- Add `--chaos 20` for more varied sign layouts
- For the wide establishing shot use `--ar 21:9` and drop the figure entirely

**Tip:** `--style raw` matters here — without it the neon gets over-saturated and the rain streaks turn painterly.

posts.title: Grok Retro Arcade Poster Series
posts.content:
A repeatable template for a matched poster series. Swap only the game concept line and the two palette colors — everything else stays fixed so the set looks coherent.

```
1980s arcade cabinet marquee poster for a fictional game called "{GAME}",
{CONCEPT LINE}, airbrushed illustration style, chrome bevel title lettering,
starburst highlights, {COLOR A} and {COLOR B} palette on deep black,
subtle print halftone texture, 2:3 poster
```

## The five in this set

1. `NEON VIPER` — a chrome snake coiled around a joystick, orange and violet
2. `STAR HAULER` — a cargo ship escaping a collapsing sun, cyan and gold
3. `RIFT RUNNER` — a skater grinding a lightning bolt, amber and crimson
4. `MECHA GARDEN` — robot hands cradling a glowing bonsai, purple and mint
5. `DEEP SIGNAL` — a diver reaching for a glowing radio tower, teal and sand

Grok keeps the lettering style consistent across a session, so generate the whole set in one conversation.

posts.title: Kling 3 Drone Flight Through Slot Canyon
posts.content:
A 10-second FPV-style shot that Kling 3 nails about 7 times out of 10. Camera language is everything — tell it what the camera does, not what the video "feels like".

```
FPV drone shot flying fast through a narrow sandstone slot canyon, camera
banking left and right following the curves of the rock, warm sunlight
shafts cutting through dust from above, motion blur on canyon walls,
smooth continuous forward motion, 10 seconds, no cuts
```

## What matters

1. `no cuts` prevents it from editing in a second angle halfway through
2. `smooth continuous forward motion` stops the drift-and-hover problem
3. Keep subjects out of it — people in fast FPV shots still warp badly
4. Generate at 10s and trim; the first second is usually a rough ramp-in

posts.title: Minimal Geometric Logo Explorations
posts.content:
Text-only post — the outputs are client work I can't share, but the prompt structure is reusable.

## Midjourney

```
flat vector logo mark, abstract geometric fox constructed from three
overlapping circles, single color on white, negative space, swiss design,
no text --ar 1:1 --v 7 --style raw --no gradients, shading, 3d
```

The `--no gradients, shading, 3d` block is what keeps it actually flat.

## ChatGPT Image 2

```
Design a minimal logo mark: an abstract fox built from exactly three
overlapping circles. Single solid black on pure white. Flat vector style,
no gradients, no outlines, no text. Center it with generous margins.
```

ChatGPT is better at "exactly three circles" — Midjourney treats counts as a suggestion. Midjourney wins on negative-space cleverness. Run both and cherry-pick.

posts.title: Luxury Perfume Product Shot
posts.content:
ChatGPT Image 2 handles glass refraction surprisingly well if you describe the lighting rig instead of the mood.

```
Studio product photograph of an amber glass perfume bottle with a brushed
gold cap, sitting on a wet black marble slab. Single softbox key light from
the upper left, white bounce card on the right, thin rim light separating
the bottle from a charcoal background. Condensation droplets on the glass.
Sharp focus on the label, f/8 depth of field. No text on the label.
```

## Notes

- Describing the actual lighting setup (key, bounce, rim) beats adjectives like "dramatic" or "cinematic"
- Ask for `no text on the label` — otherwise it invents a garbled brand name
- Follow up with "same scene, camera 30 degrees lower, closer crop" to get a matched set

posts.title: Seedance 2.0 Liquid Chrome Morph
posts.content:
Image-to-video workflow. Generate the still first (frame attached), then feed it to Seedance 2.0 with the motion prompt.

## Still (any image model)

```
macro shot of a sphere of liquid chrome hovering above a dark studio floor,
perfect mirror reflections of unseen colored lights, dust particles in the
air, black background, 85mm lens
```

## Motion prompt

```
the chrome sphere slowly deforms into a rippling liquid ribbon, twisting in
place, reflections sliding across its surface, camera locked off, studio
lights flickering between magenta and cyan, seamless loop
```

- `camera locked off` is doing the heavy lifting — Seedance loves to orbit and it ruins the loop
- Ask for `seamless loop` and generate 5s; the loop point lands around 4.2s
